celebrity.en: a famous person, especially in entertainment, sports, or media

ex.en: A celebrity was spotted at the airport this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 공항에서 한 유명인이 목격되었다.

cell.en: the smallest basic unit that makes up living things

ex.en: The human body is made up of trillions of cells.
ex.ko: 인체는 수조 개의 세포로 이루어져 있다.

cell.en: a small locked room where a prisoner is kept

ex.en: The prisoner spent ten years in a small cell.
ex.ko: 그 죄수는 좁은 감방에서 10년을 보냈다.

cell.en: (informal, especially American English) a mobile phone

ex.en: I left my cell at home by mistake.
ex.ko: 나는 실수로 휴대전화를 집에 두고 왔다.

cell.en: a device that produces electric current through a chemical reaction

ex.en: The remote control needs a new cell.
ex.ko: 그 리모컨은 새 건전지가 필요하다.

central.en: in or near the middle of a place

ex.en: The hotel is in a very central location.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 매우 중심적인 위치에 있다.

ex.en: Trust is central to any good relationship.
ex.ko: 신뢰는 좋은 관계에서 핵심적이다.

central heating.en: a heating system that warms a whole building from one central source

ex.en: The old house didn't have central heating.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 집에는 중앙난방이 없었다.

ceremony.en: a formal event held to mark an important occasion

ex.en: They held a small wedding ceremony in the garden.
ex.ko: 그들은 정원에서 작은 결혼식을 올렸다.

certainty.en: the state of being completely sure about something

ex.en: There is no certainty that the plan will work.
ex.ko: 그 계획이 성공하리라는 확실성은 없다.

challenging.cue: 능력을 다 써야 할 만큼 어려운
challenging.en: difficult in a way that tests your skill or ability

ex.en: Learning a new language can be challenging.
ex.ko: 새로운 언어를 배우는 것은 힘들 수 있다.

champion.en: a person who has won first place in a competition

ex.en: She became the world champion in swimming.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수영 세계 챔피언이 되었다.

champion.en: support or fight for a cause or person

ex.en: The senator has long championed workers' rights.
ex.ko: 그 상원의원은 오랫동안 노동자의 권리를 옹호해 왔다.

channel.en: a television station and its programs

ex.en: She changed the channel to watch the news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 뉴스를 보려고 채널을 바꿨다.

channel.en: a means through which information or help is passed

ex.en: We need to find a better channel for customer feedback.
ex.ko: 고객 의견을 받을 더 나은 경로를 찾아야 한다.

channel.en: direct energy, money, or effort toward a particular purpose

ex.en: He channeled his anger into painting.
ex.ko: 그는 분노를 그림 그리는 데 쏟았다.

chaos.en: a state of complete confusion and disorder

ex.en: The power outage caused chaos in the city.
ex.ko: 정전으로 도시는 혼란에 빠졌다.

characteristic.en: typical of a particular person or thing

ex.en: Curiosity is characteristic of young children.
ex.ko: 호기심은 어린아이들의 특징적인 모습이다.

characteristic.en: a quality that is typical of someone or something

ex.en: Kindness is one of her best characteristics.
ex.ko: 친절함은 그녀의 가장 좋은 특징 중 하나다.

characterize.en: describe the typical qualities of someone or something

ex.en: The report characterizes the region as politically unstable.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 그 지역을 정치적으로 불안정하다고 특징짓는다.

chariot.en: an old vehicle with two wheels pulled by horses, used in battles or ceremonies

ex.en: The king rode into the city on a golden chariot.
ex.ko: 왕은 황금 전차를 타고 도시로 들어왔다.

charity.en: an organization that helps people in need

ex.en: She donates money to a local charity every month.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매달 지역 자선단체에 돈을 기부한다.

charity.en: kindness or help given to people in need

ex.en: He acted out of charity, not obligation.
ex.ko: 그는 의무감이 아니라 자비심에서 행동했다.

charm.en: a quality that makes someone or something attractive and pleasing

ex.en: The old town has a certain charm.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 마을은 어떤 매력을 지니고 있다.

charm.en: a small object believed to bring good luck

ex.en: She wears a lucky charm around her neck.
ex.ko: 그녀는 목에 행운의 부적을 걸고 다닌다.

charm.en: attract or please someone with charm

ex.en: He charmed everyone at the party.
ex.ko: 그는 파티에서 모두를 매혹했다.

chase.en: run after someone or something to catch them

ex.en: The dog chased the cat up a tree.
ex.ko: 개가 고양이를 나무 위까지 쫓아갔다.

chase.en: the act of chasing someone or something

ex.en: The car chase ended when the driver crashed.
ex.ko: 운전자가 사고를 내면서 자동차 추격전이 끝났다.

chat show.en: (British English) a television or radio program where guests are interviewed about various topics

ex.en: She was interviewed on a popular chat show.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인기 있는 토크쇼에서 인터뷰를 했다.

check-in counter.en: the place at an airport or hotel where you check in

ex.en: There was a long line at the check-in counter.
ex.ko: 체크인 카운터에 긴 줄이 있었다.

check-in desk.en: a desk at an airport or hotel where you check in

ex.en: Please go to the check-in desk with your passport.
ex.ko: 여권을 가지고 체크인 데스크로 가세요.

checkout.en: the place in a store where you pay for what you are buying

ex.en: There was a long line at the checkout.
ex.ko: 계산대에 긴 줄이 있었다.

checkout.en: the time by which you must leave a hotel room

ex.en: Checkout is at eleven in the morning.
ex.ko: 체크아웃은 오전 11시다.

ex.en: She greeted us with a cheerful smile.
ex.ko: 그녀는 쾌활한 미소로 우리를 맞아주었다.

ex.en: The chemist worked in the university lab.
ex.ko: 그 화학자는 대학 실험실에서 일했다.

chemist.en: (British English) a pharmacist, or the shop where they work

ex.en: I bought some medicine at the chemist's.
ex.ko: 나는 약국에서 약을 좀 샀다.

chemistry.en: the science that studies substances and how they react with each other

ex.en: She is studying chemistry at university.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학에서 화학을 공부하고 있다.

chemistry.en: a strong natural connection or attraction between people

ex.en: The two actors had great chemistry on screen.
ex.ko: 그 두 배우는 화면에서 케미가 정말 좋았다.

cherish.en: care for someone or something deeply and hold them dear

ex.en: She cherishes the memories of her childhood.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어린 시절의 추억을 소중히 여긴다.

chest of drawers.en: a piece of furniture with drawers for storing clothes

ex.en: She keeps her sweaters in the chest of drawers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 스웨터를 서랍장에 보관한다.

chewing gum.en: a soft sweet substance that you chew but do not swallow

ex.en: He was chewing gum during class.
ex.ko: 그는 수업 중에 껌을 씹고 있었다.

chief.en: the person with the highest authority in a group or organization

ex.en: She is the chief of the fire department.
ex.ko: 그녀는 소방서의 최고 책임자다.

ex.en: The chief reason for the delay was bad weather.
ex.ko: 지연의 주된 이유는 악천후였다.

choir.en: a group of people who sing together, often in a church

ex.en: She sings in the school choir.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학교 합창단에서 노래를 부른다.

choke.en: have great difficulty breathing because something is blocking your throat

ex.en: He choked on a piece of bread.
ex.ko: 그는 빵 한 조각에 목이 메었다.

choke.en: block or become blocked so nothing can pass through

ex.en: Weeds choked the narrow stream.
ex.ko: 잡초가 좁은 개울을 막았다.

choke.en: (informal) fail to perform well because of nervousness at an important moment

ex.en: She choked in the final round of the competition.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대회 마지막 라운드에서 긴장해서 무너졌다.

Christian.en: relating to Christianity or its followers

ex.en: They got married in a Christian church.
ex.ko: 그들은 기독교 교회에서 결혼했다.

Christian.en: a person who believes in and follows Christianity

ex.en: Her grandmother is a devout Christian.
ex.ko: 그녀의 할머니는 독실한 기독교인이다.

chronological.en: arranged in the order in which events happened

ex.en: The museum displays the paintings in chronological order.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 그림들을 연대순으로 전시한다.

ex.en: He gave a little chuckle at the joke.
ex.ko: 그는 그 농담에 작게 낄낄 웃었다.

ex.en: She chuckled as she read the message.
ex.ko: 그녀는 메시지를 읽으며 낄낄 웃었다.

circular.en: having the shape of a circle

ex.en: They sat around a circular table.
ex.ko: 그들은 원형 탁자에 둘러앉았다.

circular.en: a printed advertisement distributed widely to many people

ex.en: A circular about the sale arrived in the mail.
ex.ko: 세일에 관한 광고 전단지가 우편으로 도착했다.

circus.en: a traveling show with performers and trained animals

ex.en: We took the kids to the circus last weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 지난 주말 아이들을 데리고 서커스에 갔다.

citizenship.en: the legal right to belong to a particular country

ex.en: She applied for British citizenship.
ex.ko: 그녀는 영국 시민권을 신청했다.

civil.en: relating to ordinary citizens, not the military or government

ex.en: The country returned to civil rule after the war.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 전쟁 후 민간 통치로 돌아갔다.

ex.en: Please try to be civil to each other.
ex.ko: 서로에게 예의를 지켜 주세요.

civilization.en: a human society with an advanced level of development in culture, government, and technology

ex.en: Ancient Egypt was one of the world's earliest civilizations.
ex.ko: 고대 이집트는 세계에서 가장 오래된 문명 중 하나였다.

classical.en: relating to serious traditional music such as symphonies

ex.en: She prefers classical music to pop.
ex.ko: 그녀는 팝보다 클래식 음악을 더 좋아한다.

classical.en: relating to ancient Greek or Roman culture and literature

ex.en: He studied classical literature at university.
ex.ko: 그는 대학에서 고전 문학을 공부했다.

classify.en: arrange things into groups according to their type

ex.en: Books in the library are classified by subject.
ex.ko: 도서관의 책들은 주제별로 분류된다.

ex.en: The government classified the documents as top secret.
ex.ko: 정부는 그 문서들을 일급비밀로 분류했다.

cliff.en: a high steep rock face, especially next to the sea

ex.en: They watched the sunset from the cliff.
ex.ko: 그들은 절벽에서 일몰을 바라보았다.

climate.en: the general weather conditions typical of an area over a long time

ex.en: The region has a warm, dry climate.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 따뜻하고 건조한 기후를 가지고 있다.

climate.en: the general attitude or mood in a society at a particular time

ex.en: The current economic climate makes it hard to find a job.
ex.ko: 현재의 경제적 분위기 속에서는 일자리를 구하기 어렵다.

climber.en: a person who climbs mountains or rocks as a sport

ex.en: The climber reached the summit before noon.
ex.ko: 그 등반가는 정오 전에 정상에 도달했다.

climber.en: a plant that grows by climbing up a support or other plants

ex.en: Ivy is a common climber found on old walls.
ex.ko: 담쟁이덩굴은 오래된 벽에서 흔히 볼 수 있는 덩굴 식물이다.

clinic.en: a place where people go to get medical treatment, often for a specific area of health

ex.en: She works at a dental clinic downtown.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시내에 있는 치과 클리닉에서 일한다.

clinic.en: a session where people receive instruction or practice in a particular skill

ex.en: He attended a tennis clinic over the summer.
ex.ko: 그는 여름 동안 테니스 강습회에 참가했다.

clip.en: a small device used to hold papers or things together

ex.en: She attached the papers with a clip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 서류를 클립으로 고정했다.

clip.en: cut something, especially hair or a small piece, with scissors or shears

ex.en: He clipped the hedge in the garden.
ex.ko: 그는 정원의 산울타리를 깎았다.

clip.en: a short section taken from a film, video, or piece of music

ex.en: She shared a funny video clip online.
ex.ko: 그녀는 재미있는 영상 클립을 온라인에 공유했다.

closely.en: in a way that is closely connected or related

ex.en: The two issues are closely linked.
ex.ko: 그 두 문제는 서로 밀접하게 연관되어 있다.

ex.en: Please read the contract closely before signing.
ex.ko: 서명하기 전에 계약서를 자세히 읽어 보세요.

coal.en: a black rock dug from the ground and burned as fuel

ex.en: The old factory was powered by coal.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 공장은 석탄으로 가동되었다.

coastal.en: located on or near a coast

ex.en: Many tourists visit the coastal towns in summer.
ex.ko: 많은 관광객이 여름에 해안 마을을 방문한다.

cod.en: a large sea fish with white flesh, often eaten as food

ex.en: We had fish and chips made with cod.
ex.ko: 우리는 대구로 만든 피시앤칩스를 먹었다.

coffin.en: a box in which a dead body is buried or cremated

ex.en: The coffin was carried by six men.
ex.ko: 그 관은 여섯 명의 남자가 운구했다.

collapse.en: fall down suddenly, often because of damage or weakness

ex.en: The old bridge collapsed during the storm.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 다리는 폭풍 중에 무너졌다.

collapse.en: fall down suddenly because of illness or exhaustion

ex.en: He collapsed from exhaustion after the race.
ex.ko: 그는 경주 후 탈진해서 쓰러졌다.

collapse.en: fail suddenly and completely, as of a business or market

ex.en: The company collapsed after years of debt.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 수년간의 빚 끝에 붕괴했다.

collar.en: the part of a shirt or coat that fits around the neck

ex.en: He wore a shirt with a stiff collar.
ex.ko: 그는 빳빳한 옷깃이 있는 셔츠를 입었다.

collar.en: a band placed around an animal's neck

ex.en: The dog's collar had a small bell on it.
ex.ko: 그 개의 목걸이에는 작은 방울이 달려 있었다.

columnist.en: a person who writes a regular column in a newspaper or magazine

ex.en: She is a well-known columnist for a national newspaper.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한 전국지의 유명한 칼럼니스트다.

combination.en: a mixture of different things or qualities used together

ex.en: The dish is a combination of sweet and sour flavors.
ex.ko: 그 요리는 달고 신맛의 조합이다.

combination.en: a sequence of numbers or letters used to open a lock

ex.en: He forgot the combination to his locker.
ex.ko: 그는 자기 사물함의 비밀번호를 잊어버렸다.

combine.en: join or mix two or more things together

ex.en: The recipe combines chocolate and orange flavors.
ex.ko: 그 레시피는 초콜릿과 오렌지 맛을 결합한다.

comedian.en: a person whose job is to make people laugh, especially by telling jokes

ex.en: The comedian had the whole audience laughing.
ex.ko: 그 코미디언은 관객 전체를 웃게 만들었다.

comedy.en: a funny movie, play, or story meant to make people laugh

ex.en: We watched a romantic comedy last night.
ex.ko: 우리는 어젯밤 로맨틱 코미디를 봤다.

comet.cue: 긴 꼬리를 끌며 우주를 도는 천체
comet.en: a bright object in space made of ice and dust that travels around the sun

ex.en: You could see the comet clearly in the night sky.
ex.ko: 밤하늘에서 그 혜성이 뚜렷하게 보였다.

comfort.en: a pleasant feeling of physical ease and no pain or worry

ex.en: She enjoyed the comfort of her warm bed.
ex.ko: 그녀는 따뜻한 침대의 편안함을 즐겼다.

comfort.en: help someone feel less sad or worried

ex.en: He tried to comfort her after the bad news.
ex.ko: 그는 나쁜 소식을 들은 그녀를 위로하려 했다.

comfort.en: something that makes sadness or difficulty easier to bear

ex.en: Her friends were a great comfort during that difficult time.
ex.ko: 그 힘든 시기에 친구들이 큰 위안이 되었다.

comma.en: the punctuation mark (,) used to separate parts of a sentence

ex.en: Don't forget to put a comma before 'and' in a list.
ex.ko: 목록에서 'and' 앞에 쉼표 넣는 것을 잊지 마세요.

command.en: an official order to do something

ex.en: The soldiers obeyed the general's command.
ex.ko: 병사들은 장군의 명령에 복종했다.

command.en: give an official order to do something

ex.en: The captain commanded the crew to abandon ship.
ex.ko: 선장은 선원들에게 배를 버리라고 명령했다.

command.en: a thorough knowledge or skillful use of something, such as a language

ex.en: She has an excellent command of French.
ex.ko: 그녀는 프랑스어를 뛰어나게 구사한다.

command.en: an instruction given to a computer to perform a task

ex.en: Type the command to run the program.
ex.ko: 프로그램을 실행하려면 명령어를 입력하세요.

ex.en: He refused to comment on the rumors.
ex.ko: 그는 그 소문에 대해 논평하기를 거부했다.

comment.en: a written or spoken remark expressing an opinion

ex.en: She left a nice comment on my photo.
ex.ko: 그녀는 내 사진에 좋은 댓글을 남겼다.

commercial.en: an advertisement shown on television or radio

ex.en: I always skip the commercials.
ex.ko: 나는 항상 광고를 건너뛴다.

commercial.en: relating to the buying and selling of goods and services

ex.en: The area is full of commercial buildings.
ex.ko: 그 지역에는 상업용 건물이 가득하다.

ex.en: He was accused of committing a crime.
ex.ko: 그는 범죄를 저질렀다는 혐의를 받았다.

commit.en: promise or dedicate yourself fully to something

ex.en: She committed herself to finishing the project on time.
ex.ko: 그녀는 프로젝트를 제때 끝내는 데 전념했다.

common sense.en: the basic ability to make good, practical decisions

ex.en: Use common sense when crossing the street.
ex.ko: 길을 건널 때는 상식을 발휘하세요.

ex.en: This mistake is commonly made by beginners.
ex.ko: 이 실수는 초보자들이 흔히 저지른다.

companion.en: a person who spends time with or accompanies you, especially on a journey

ex.en: The old man's dog was his only companion.
ex.ko: 그 노인의 개는 그의 유일한 동반자였다.

comparison.en: the act of examining how two or more things are similar or different

ex.en: There's no comparison between the two restaurants.
ex.ko: 그 두 식당은 비교할 수가 없다.

compete.en: try to win or do better than others

ex.en: Ten athletes competed for the gold medal.
ex.ko: 10명의 선수가 금메달을 놓고 경쟁했다.

competitive.en: having a strong desire to win or be the best

ex.en: He's a very competitive person, even in games with friends.
ex.ko: 그는 친구들과의 게임에서도 매우 경쟁심이 강하다.

competitive.cue: 다른 것과 겨룰 만큼 좋은 가격·조건
competitive.en: as good as or better than others in price or quality

ex.en: The store offers competitive prices on electronics.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 전자제품에 경쟁력 있는 가격을 제공한다.

competitor.en: a person or company competing against others

ex.en: Our biggest competitor just lowered its prices.
ex.ko: 우리의 가장 큰 경쟁자가 방금 가격을 낮췄다.

complement.cue: 서로 부족한 점을 채워 완전하게 하다
complement.en: make something complete or better by adding something else

ex.en: The wine complements the meal perfectly.
ex.ko: 그 와인은 식사를 완벽하게 보완해 준다.

complement.en: a word or phrase that completes the meaning of a sentence

ex.en: In the sentence 'She is happy,' 'happy' is the complement.
ex.ko: 'She is happy'라는 문장에서 'happy'는 보어다.

ex.en: I completely forgot about the meeting.
ex.ko: 나는 그 회의를 완전히 잊어버렸다.

complex.en: made up of many different parts and difficult to understand

ex.en: The tax system is extremely complex.
ex.ko: 그 세금 제도는 매우 복잡하다.

complex.en: a group of buildings used for a particular purpose

ex.en: They built a new sports complex in the city.
ex.ko: 그들은 도시에 새 스포츠 단지를 지었다.

complex.en: a set of strong, often unconscious feelings that affect someone's behavior

ex.en: She has a complex about her height.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 키에 대해 콤플렉스를 가지고 있다.

complicate.en: make something more difficult to deal with

ex.en: His late arrival complicated our plans.
ex.ko: 그가 늦게 도착해서 우리 계획이 복잡해졌다.

complicated.en: difficult to understand because of having many parts

ex.en: It's a complicated situation with no easy answer.
ex.ko: 그것은 쉬운 답이 없는 복잡한 상황이다.

compliment.en: a remark that expresses praise or admiration

ex.en: She gave me a nice compliment on my haircut.
ex.ko: 그녀는 내 헤어스타일에 대해 좋은 칭찬을 해주었다.

compliment.en: praise someone for something they have done or said

ex.en: He complimented her on her cooking.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 요리 솜씨를 칭찬했다.

ex.en: She composed a beautiful melody for the film.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 영화를 위해 아름다운 멜로디를 작곡했다.

compose.en: form or make up a whole

ex.en: Water is composed of hydrogen and oxygen.
ex.ko: 물은 수소와 산소로 구성되어 있다.

compose.en: make yourself calm, especially after being upset

ex.en: She took a deep breath to compose herself.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마음을 가라앉히려고 심호흡을 했다.

composition.en: a short piece of writing done as an exercise or assignment

ex.en: We had to write a composition about our summer vacation.
ex.ko: 우리는 여름방학에 관한 작문을 써야 했다.

composition.en: the way in which the parts of something are put together

ex.en: The composition of the committee changed after the election.
ex.ko: 그 위원회의 구성은 선거 이후 바뀌었다.

compound.en: a substance made from two or more elements chemically joined together

ex.en: Water is a chemical compound of hydrogen and oxygen.
ex.ko: 물은 수소와 산소의 화학 화합물이다.

compound.en: made up of two or more parts

ex.en: 'Sunflower' is a compound word.
ex.ko: 'Sunflower'는 합성어이다.

compound.en: make a problem or difficult situation worse

ex.en: The heavy rain compounded the flood damage.
ex.ko: 폭우가 홍수 피해를 더욱 악화시켰다.

compromise.en: reach an agreement by each side giving up some demands

ex.en: They compromised and split the cost evenly.
ex.ko: 그들은 타협해서 비용을 똑같이 나눴다.

compromise.en: an agreement reached by each side giving up some demands

ex.en: They reached a compromise after hours of discussion.
ex.ko: 그들은 몇 시간의 논의 끝에 타협안에 도달했다.

compromise.en: put something such as safety or principles at risk

ex.en: He refused to compromise his safety for the deadline.
ex.ko: 그는 마감일 때문에 자신의 안전을 위태롭게 하고 싶지 않았다.

concentration.en: the ability to give your full attention to one thing

ex.en: The noise outside broke her concentration.
ex.ko: 바깥의 소음이 그녀의 집중을 방해했다.

concentration.en: the amount of a substance present in a certain amount of liquid

ex.en: The concentration of salt in seawater is quite high.
ex.ko: 바닷물의 소금 농도는 꽤 높다.

concept.en: a general idea or principle related to something abstract

ex.en: The concept of time is different in each culture.
ex.ko: 시간이라는 개념은 문화마다 다르다.

ex.en: She looked concerned when she heard the news.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 소식을 듣고 걱정스러운 표정을 지었다.

concerned.en: involved in or affected by something

ex.en: All the parties concerned agreed to the plan.
ex.ko: 관련된 모든 당사자가 그 계획에 동의했다.

conclude.en: decide something after considering all the facts

ex.en: The police concluded that the fire was an accident.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 화재가 사고였다고 결론지었다.

ex.en: He concluded his speech with a joke.
ex.ko: 그는 농담으로 연설을 마쳤다.

conclusion.en: a final decision or judgment reached after considering the facts

ex.en: We came to the conclusion that the plan wouldn't work.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 계획이 성공하지 못할 것이라는 결론에 도달했다.

conclusion.en: the final part of something, such as a speech or story

ex.en: The essay's conclusion summarizes the main points.
ex.ko: 그 에세이의 결말은 주요 요점을 요약한다.

conduct.en: direct the performance of musicians, especially an orchestra

ex.en: She conducted the orchestra at the concert.
ex.ko: 그녀는 콘서트에서 오케스트라를 지휘했다.

conduct.en: organize and carry out an activity such as research or a meeting

ex.en: The company conducted a survey of its customers.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 고객을 대상으로 설문 조사를 실시했다.

ex.en: His conduct during the meeting was very professional.
ex.ko: 회의 중 그의 행동은 매우 전문적이었다.

conduct.en: allow heat or electricity to pass through

ex.en: Copper conducts electricity very well.
ex.ko: 구리는 전기를 매우 잘 전도한다.

confidence.en: a feeling of trust in your own abilities

ex.en: She answered the questions with confidence.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신감 있게 질문에 답했다.

confidence.en: a feeling of trust in someone or something

ex.en: I have full confidence in your abilities.
ex.ko: 나는 네 능력을 전적으로 신뢰한다.

confidence.en: something told to someone privately and secretly

ex.en: She told me the secret in strict confidence.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 비밀을 나에게 극비로 털어놓았다.

confirm.en: state that something is true or that it will happen

ex.en: She called to confirm the reservation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 예약을 확인하려고 전화했다.

confirmation.en: a statement or action proving that something is true or will happen

ex.en: We received confirmation of our flight booking.
ex.ko: 우리는 항공편 예약 확인을 받았다.

conflict.en: a serious disagreement or fight between people or groups

ex.en: The conflict between the two countries lasted for years.
ex.ko: 그 두 나라 사이의 갈등은 수년간 지속되었다.

conflict.en: a struggle between opposing feelings or ideas inside a person

ex.en: She felt a conflict between her career and her family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 일과 가족 사이에서 갈등을 느꼈다.

conflict.en: happen at the same time as something else, causing a problem

ex.en: The meeting conflicts with my doctor's appointment.
ex.ko: 그 회의는 내 병원 예약과 겹친다.

confusing.en: difficult to understand and causing confusion

ex.en: The instructions were confusing and hard to follow.
ex.ko: 그 설명서는 혼란스럽고 따라 하기 어려웠다.

confusion.en: a state of not understanding what is happening or what to do

ex.en: There was a lot of confusion at the airport due to the delays.
ex.ko: 지연 때문에 공항은 큰 혼란에 빠졌다.

confusion.en: a mistake caused by thinking one thing is another

ex.en: There seems to be some confusion about the meeting time.
ex.ko: 회의 시간에 대해 약간의 착오가 있는 것 같다.

congratulate.en: tell someone that you are happy about their success or good news

ex.en: I congratulated her on passing the exam.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 시험에 합격한 것을 축하했다.

connect.en: join two or more things together

ex.en: Connect the two wires to turn on the light.
ex.ko: 불을 켜려면 두 전선을 연결하세요.

connect.en: form a good relationship or understanding with someone

ex.en: She really connected with her new classmates.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 반 친구들과 정말 잘 통했다.

connect.en: see or show a link between two things or ideas

ex.en: Researchers connect the disease to poor diet.
ex.ko: 연구자들은 그 질병을 부실한 식습관과 관련짓는다.

connection.en: a relationship between two things or people

ex.en: There's a clear connection between exercise and good health.
ex.ko: 운동과 건강 사이에는 분명한 관계가 있다.

connection.en: a flight, train, or bus that you change to during a journey

ex.en: We missed our connection because the first flight was delayed.
ex.ko: 첫 비행기가 지연되어서 환승편을 놓쳤다.

conquer.en: take control of a country or place by force

ex.en: The empire conquered many neighboring lands.
ex.ko: 그 제국은 많은 이웃 땅을 정복했다.

conquer.en: successfully deal with or overcome a problem or fear

ex.en: She finally conquered her fear of flying.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마침내 비행 공포를 극복했다.

conscious.en: awake and aware of what is happening around you

ex.en: The patient was still conscious after the accident.
ex.ko: 환자는 사고 후에도 여전히 의식이 있었다.

conscious.en: aware of and paying attention to something

ex.en: She is very conscious of her health these days.
ex.ko: 그녀는 요즘 자기 건강을 많이 의식한다.

ex.en: He missed the bus, and consequently arrived late.
ex.ko: 그는 버스를 놓쳤고, 결과적으로 늦게 도착했다.

conservation.en: the protection of the natural environment and its resources

ex.en: The organization works on wildlife conservation.
ex.ko: 그 단체는 야생동물 보호 활동을 한다.

conservation.en: the act of keeping something in its original state, preventing damage or loss

ex.en: The museum focuses on the conservation of ancient artifacts.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 고대 유물 보존에 힘쓴다.

conservative.en: opposed to change and holding traditional values

ex.en: Her parents have quite conservative views.
ex.ko: 그녀의 부모님은 꽤 보수적인 견해를 가지고 있다.

conservative.en: cautious, tending to underestimate rather than overestimate

ex.en: A conservative estimate puts the cost at one million dollars.
ex.ko: 신중하게 추정해도 비용은 백만 달러에 이른다.

considerable.en: large in amount, size, or degree

ex.en: The project will take a considerable amount of time.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 상당한 시간이 걸릴 것이다.

consideration.en: careful thought about something before making a decision

ex.en: After much consideration, she decided to accept the offer.
ex.ko: 많은 고려 끝에 그녀는 그 제안을 받아들이기로 했다.

consideration.en: thoughtful concern for the feelings or needs of others

ex.en: He always shows great consideration for his elderly neighbors.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 나이 든 이웃들을 크게 배려한다.

consonant.en: a speech sound made by partly or fully blocking the air, such as b, c, or d

ex.en: 'B' and 'k' are consonants, while 'a' and 'e' are vowels.
ex.ko: 'b'와 'k'는 자음이고, 'a'와 'e'는 모음이다.

ex.en: He is constantly checking his phone.
ex.ko: 그는 끊임없이 휴대폰을 확인한다.

constitute.en: form or make up a whole

ex.en: Women constitute more than half of the university's students.
ex.ko: 여성이 그 대학 학생의 절반 이상을 차지한다.

constitute.en: formally establish something, such as an organization or law

ex.en: The committee was constituted to review the policy.
ex.ko: 그 정책을 검토하기 위해 위원회가 설립되었다.

constitution.en: the basic set of laws that states the rights of citizens and the powers of a government

ex.en: The right to free speech is protected by the constitution.
ex.ko: 표현의 자유에 대한 권리는 헌법으로 보호된다.

constitution.en: the physical health and strength of a person's body

ex.en: She has a strong constitution and rarely gets sick.
ex.ko: 그녀는 체질이 강해서 좀처럼 아프지 않는다.

construct.en: build something, such as a building or road

ex.en: They are constructing a new bridge over the river.
ex.ko: 그들은 강 위에 새 다리를 건설하고 있다.

construct.en: an idea or theory containing various conceptual elements, typically one considered to be subjective and not based in reality

ex.en: Some argue that race is a social construct.
ex.ko: 어떤 사람들은 인종이 사회적 개념이라고 주장한다.

construction.en: the process of building something, such as a road or building

ex.en: The construction of the new hospital will take two years.
ex.ko: 새 병원의 건설에는 2년이 걸릴 것이다.

construction.en: a group of words forming a grammatical unit within a sentence

ex.en: This grammatical construction is common in formal writing.
ex.ko: 이 문법 구문은 격식체 글에서 흔히 쓰인다.

consume.en: use up resources, energy, or products

ex.en: This car consumes a lot of fuel.
ex.ko: 이 차는 연료를 많이 소비한다.

consume.en: destroy something completely, especially by fire

ex.en: The fire quickly consumed the old barn.
ex.ko: 불이 빠르게 그 낡은 헛간을 태워 없앴다.

consumer.en: a person who buys and uses goods or services

ex.en: Consumers are demanding more eco-friendly products.
ex.ko: 소비자들은 더 친환경적인 제품을 요구하고 있다.

consumption.en: the amount of something used, or the act of using it

ex.en: The country's energy consumption has increased sharply.
ex.ko: 그 나라의 에너지 소비량이 급격히 증가했다.

contain.en: have something inside or include something as a part

ex.en: This drink contains a lot of sugar.
ex.ko: 이 음료는 설탕을 많이 포함하고 있다.

contain.en: control or limit something, preventing it from spreading further

ex.en: Firefighters worked to contain the wildfire.
ex.ko: 소방관들은 그 산불을 저지하려고 애썼다.

content.en: satisfied with what you have; not wanting more

ex.en: She seems content with her simple life.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 소박한 삶에 만족하는 것 같다.

content.en: the information or ideas contained in something such as a book, speech, or website

ex.en: The content of the report was very detailed.
ex.ko: 그 보고서의 내용은 매우 자세했다.

content.en: the amount of a substance contained in something

ex.en: This cereal has a high sugar content.
ex.ko: 이 시리얼은 당 함량이 높다.

continual.en: happening again and again, often with short breaks in between

ex.en: The company has faced continual problems with its software.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 소프트웨어와 관련해 지속적인 문제를 겪어 왔다.

continually.en: again and again, often with breaks in between

ex.en: He continually interrupted her while she was speaking.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀가 말하는 동안 계속해서 끼어들었다.

continuous.en: happening or existing without any breaks or gaps

ex.en: The patient needs continuous care.
ex.ko: 그 환자는 계속적인 간호가 필요하다.

ex.en: The machine has been running continuously for a week.
ex.ko: 그 기계는 일주일 동안 끊임없이 작동해 왔다.

ex.en: She took the contrary view on the issue.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 문제에 대해 반대되는 견해를 취했다.

contrary.en: the opposite (used in phrases like 'on the contrary' or 'contrary to')

ex.en: Contrary to popular belief, bats are not blind.
ex.ko: 일반적인 믿음과 달리, 박쥐는 눈이 멀지 않았다.

contribute.en: give money, help, or other things to a common effort or cause

ex.en: Everyone contributed money to the charity.
ex.ko: 모두가 그 자선단체에 돈을 기부했다.

contribute.cue: 어떤 결과의 원인 중 하나가 되다
contribute.en: be one of the causes of something happening

ex.en: Stress can contribute to health problems.
ex.ko: 스트레스는 건강 문제의 원인이 될 수 있다.

contribution.en: something you do or give that helps achieve something

ex.en: Her contribution to the project was invaluable.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트에 대한 그녀의 기여는 매우 소중했다.

contribution.en: money given to help a cause or charity

ex.en: They asked for contributions to help rebuild the school.
ex.ko: 그들은 학교 재건을 돕기 위한 기부금을 요청했다.

controversial.en: causing a lot of disagreement or argument

ex.en: The new law is highly controversial.
ex.ko: 그 새 법은 논란이 매우 많다.

convey.en: communicate an idea, feeling, or message to someone

ex.en: Her smile conveyed a sense of relief.
ex.ko: 그녀의 미소는 안도감을 전달했다.

convey.en: carry or transport someone or something from one place to another

ex.en: The pipeline conveys oil across the country.
ex.ko: 그 송유관은 전국에 걸쳐 석유를 운반한다.

convince.en: make someone believe that something is true

ex.en: He convinced me to try the new restaurant.
ex.ko: 그는 나에게 그 새 식당을 시도해 보라고 설득했다.

copyright.en: the legal right to control the use of a creative work, such as a book or song

ex.en: You need permission to use copyrighted music.
ex.ko: 저작권이 있는 음악을 사용하려면 허가가 필요하다.

correction.en: a change made to fix a mistake

ex.en: She made a few corrections to the report.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 보고서에 몇 가지 수정을 가했다.

correction.en: a drop in stock prices after a period of increase

ex.en: Analysts expect a market correction soon.
ex.ko: 분석가들은 곧 시장 조정이 있을 것으로 예상한다.

cotton.en: a soft natural fiber used to make cloth, or the plant it comes from

ex.en: This shirt is made of 100% cotton.
ex.ko: 이 셔츠는 100% 면으로 만들어졌다.

cough.en: push air out of your throat suddenly and noisily, often because of illness

ex.en: He coughed several times during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의 중에 여러 번 기침을 했다.

cough.en: an act or sound of coughing

ex.en: She has a bad cough and a sore throat.
ex.ko: 그녀는 심한 기침과 목 통증이 있다.

council.en: a group of people elected or chosen to manage or advise on local affairs

ex.en: The city council voted to build a new park.
ex.ko: 시의회는 새 공원을 짓기로 표결했다.

counseling.en: professional advice given to help someone with personal or psychological problems

ex.en: She decided to get counseling after the divorce.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이혼 후 상담을 받기로 했다.

counter.en: a long flat surface where you pay for goods or are served

ex.en: She placed her items on the counter.
ex.ko: 그녀는 물건들을 카운터 위에 올려놓았다.

counter.en: respond to an argument, criticism, or attack

ex.en: She countered his argument with strong evidence.
ex.ko: 그녀는 확실한 증거로 그의 주장에 반박했다.

counter.en: in the opposite direction to, or in contrast with

ex.en: His actions ran counter to what he had promised.
ex.ko: 그의 행동은 그가 약속했던 것과 반대로 흘러갔다.

ex.en: She has answered countless emails today.
ex.ko: 그녀는 오늘 셀 수 없이 많은 이메일에 답했다.

county.en: an area of a country or state with its own local government

ex.en: She grew up in a small county in the countryside.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시골의 작은 군에서 자랐다.

courage.en: the ability to face danger, pain, or difficulty without giving in to fear

ex.en: It took a lot of courage to speak up.
ex.ko: 목소리를 내는 데는 많은 용기가 필요했다.

ex.en: The courageous firefighter saved the child from the fire.
ex.ko: 그 용감한 소방관은 불 속에서 아이를 구했다.

courgette.en: (British English) a long green vegetable, also called zucchini in American English

ex.en: She sliced the courgette for the salad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 샐러드용으로 애호박을 썰었다.

craft.en: make something skillfully, especially by hand

ex.en: The artist crafted a beautiful vase from clay.
ex.ko: 그 예술가는 점토로 아름다운 꽃병을 정교하게 만들었다.

craft.en: a skill, especially one involving making things with your hands

ex.en: Pottery is an ancient craft.
ex.ko: 도자기 공예는 오래된 기술이다.

ex.en: Several small craft were seen near the coast.
ex.ko: 해안 근처에서 작은 배 몇 척이 목격되었다.

crash.en: hit something violently, or fall down suddenly and violently

ex.en: The car crashed into a tree.
ex.ko: 그 차는 나무에 충돌했다.

crash.en: an accident in which a vehicle hits something

ex.en: Three people were injured in the car crash.
ex.ko: 그 자동차 충돌 사고로 세 명이 다쳤다.

crash.en: (of a computer or program) suddenly stop working

ex.en: My computer crashed while I was saving the file.
ex.ko: 파일을 저장하는 동안 내 컴퓨터가 다운됐다.

creator.en: a person who makes or invents something

ex.en: She is the creator of a popular children's show.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인기 있는 어린이 프로그램의 창작자다.

criminal.en: a person who has committed a crime

ex.en: The police arrested the criminal near the bank.
ex.ko: 경찰은 은행 근처에서 그 범죄자를 체포했다.

criminal.en: relating to crime or its punishment

ex.en: He was found guilty of a criminal offense.
ex.ko: 그는 형사상의 범죄로 유죄 판결을 받았다.

crisis.en: a time of great danger or difficulty

ex.en: The country is going through an economic crisis.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 경제 위기를 겪고 있다.

critic.en: a person whose job is to give opinions about art, movies, books, etc.

ex.en: The film received praise from critics.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 비평가들의 찬사를 받았다.

critic.en: a person who often finds fault with someone or something

ex.en: Critics say the new policy will hurt small businesses.
ex.ko: 비판자들은 새 정책이 소상공인에게 피해를 줄 것이라고 말한다.

critical.en: expressing disapproval or pointing out faults

ex.en: He was very critical of the new policy.
ex.ko: 그는 새 정책에 매우 비판적이었다.

critical.en: extremely important, often because a decision must be made quickly

ex.en: This is a critical moment for the company.
ex.ko: 이것은 회사에 중대한 순간이다.

critical.en: (of a patient) in a very serious, life-threatening condition

ex.en: The patient is in critical condition after the accident.
ex.ko: 그 환자는 사고 후 위독한 상태다.

crop.en: a plant grown in large amounts, especially for food

ex.en: Farmers here grow crops like wheat and corn.
ex.ko: 이곳 농부들은 밀과 옥수수 같은 작물을 재배한다.

crop.en: a group of people or things that appear or are produced at the same time

ex.en: This year's crop of graduates is especially talented.
ex.ko: 올해 졸업생들은 유독 재능이 뛰어나다.

crossing.en: a place where you can cross a road, railway, or river

ex.en: Always use the pedestrian crossing when crossing the street.
ex.ko: 길을 건널 때는 항상 횡단보도를 이용하세요.

crossing.en: a journey across a sea or large body of water

ex.en: The crossing to the island took three hours.
ex.ko: 그 섬으로 건너가는 항해는 3시간이 걸렸다.

crossroads.en: a place where two or more roads meet

ex.en: Turn left at the crossroads.
ex.ko: 그 교차로에서 좌회전하세요.

crossroads.en: a point in time when an important decision must be made

ex.en: She felt she was at a crossroads in her career.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 커리어에서 갈림길에 서 있다고 느꼈다.

crow.en: a large black bird with a harsh call

ex.en: A crow perched on the fence.
ex.ko: 까마귀 한 마리가 울타리에 앉았다.

crow.en: talk boastfully about something you have achieved

ex.en: He was crowing about his victory all week.
ex.ko: 그는 일주일 내내 자신의 승리에 대해 우쭐댔다.

cruel.en: willing to cause pain or suffering to others

ex.en: It was cruel of him to laugh at her mistake.
ex.ko: 그가 그녀의 실수를 비웃은 것은 잔인했다.

crush.en: press something so hard that it breaks or is badly damaged

ex.en: The car was crushed under the fallen tree.
ex.ko: 그 차는 쓰러진 나무 아래에서 으스러졌다.

crush.en: a strong but often short-lived feeling of love, especially in a teenager

ex.en: She had a huge crush on her classmate.
ex.ko: 그녀는 반 친구를 짝사랑했다.

crush.en: a large crowd of people pressed closely together

ex.en: She got separated from her friends in the crush.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인파 속에서 친구들과 떨어지고 말았다.

cucumber.en: a long green vegetable eaten raw in salads

ex.en: She added cucumber slices to the salad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 샐러드에 오이 조각을 넣었다.

cultivate.en: prepare land and grow crops on it

ex.en: Farmers cultivate rice in this region.
ex.ko: 이 지역 농부들은 쌀을 경작한다.

cultivate.en: develop or improve a skill, quality, or relationship over time

ex.en: She has cultivated strong relationships with her clients.
ex.ko: 그녀는 고객들과 돈독한 관계를 길러 왔다.
